L1.area: Proxy request verification and authorization response handling

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where a malformed HTTP request causes the request verification service to return a successful response instead of denying access when it encounters an error processing the incoming request, allowing unauthenticated users to access protected resources.

L3.area: Forward authentication proxy verification endpoint and URL parsing error handling
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where a malformed HTTP request URI causes the forward authentication verification endpoint to return a successful HTTP response instead of an unauthorized response when the target URL cannot be parsed, allowing unauthenticated access to resources protected by a reverse proxy using nginx's auth_request module.

ground_truth.reason: When a malformed HTTP request causes URL parsing to fail in the verify endpoint, the error handler returns a 200 OK response instead of 401 Unauthorized, allowing attackers to bypass authentication entirely.
